metadata.method: Cross-Impact Analysis with STEEPs + FSSF + Three Horizons

cross_impact_matrix.description: SCOTUS ruling directly caused Trump's Section 122 pivot. The constitutional battle over trade authority creates a feedback loop between judicial, executive, and legislative branches. Together they represent a tectonic shift in US trade policy architecture.
New US tariff mechanism cascades to EU through proposed 15-20% minimum tariff. EU CPI divergence (Bavaria +1.9%, France +1.0%, Spain +2.3%) complicates unified European response.
China's AI-centered Five-Year Plan creates incentive for technology acquisition; Samsung DRAM espionage arrests illustrate the semiconductor cold war escalating. Korea caught between US and China.

Both represent nuclear proliferation challenges: Iran's verification gap and NK's 50-warhead declaration. Both reject international verification/denuclearization frameworks, weakening global nonproliferation regime.

Quantum advantage milestone and AI agent democratization represent convergent technological acceleration. Hybrid quantum-AI systems could amplify the democratization of sophisticated computational capabilities.
High Seas Treaty and SAF milestones both represent institutional/industrial sustainability transitions. They form a coherent cluster with EU renewable overtaking fossil fuels.
Japan's 'Japan First' supermajority and NK's nuclear hardline create a dangerous tension in Northeast Asian security architecture. Japan may accelerate defense spending and security posture changes.
US trade authority upheaval may shift political attention from European security commitments. Ukraine's EU accession push gains urgency as US reliability questions grow.

South Africa's fiscal turnaround and Lobito Corridor fast-tracking represent an emerging African economic infrastructure narrative, competing with China's BRI and attracting Western development capital.

Korea attracting AI investment (OpenAI/Samsung/SK data centers) while suffering semiconductor espionage losses. Dual forces of technology attraction and technology leakage define Korea's tech security dilemma.

emerging_themes.description: The SCOTUS-Trump-Section 122 sequence represents a constitutional restructuring of US trade policy. This affects every trading nation and creates legal uncertainty in global commerce.
Both Iran and North Korea are operating outside international verification frameworks. The dual challenge to nonproliferation norms represents a systemic risk to global security architecture.

emerging_themes.theme: AI Paradigm Transition: From Models to Agents and Physical AI
emerging_themes.description: Multiple signals converge on AI's transition from large language models to agentic, physical, and quantum-enhanced systems. China embedding AI in national planning, Samsung in consumer devices, and quantum computing reaching advantage milestone.
The semiconductor cold war intensifies with espionage arrests, military technology leaks, and concurrent tech investment. Korea occupies a critical node in this competition.
Ukraine pushing for 2027 accession, Iceland referendum, and citizens initiatives suggest EU enlargement and deepening are accelerating in response to security and values-driven pressures.
Multiple African signals point to economic recovery, infrastructure investment, value-added policy, and regional integration — an emerging narrative of continental economic agency.
